messages.content: Explain the difference between a list and a tuple in Python.

A list is mutable — you can add, remove, or change elements after creation. A tuple is immutable — once created, its elements cannot be modified. Tuples are slightly more memory-efficient and can be used as dictionary keys.
When should I prefer a tuple over a list?

Prefer a tuple when the data should not change (e.g. coordinates, RGB values, database records), when you need it as a dict key or set element, or when you want to signal immutability to other developers.
